etymology.description: semantic ⺼ (meat, body) + phonetic 卻 (sound) -> part of the body -> foot or leg
etymology.interpretation: The character 腳 (jiǎo, 'foot') consists of the semantic component ⺼, representing 'meat' or 'body', indicating it is a part of the body, and the phonetic component 卻, which provides the sound. Together, they depict a body part specifically referring to the foot or leg.
etymology.simplification: 脚 is a simplified variant of 腳, where the simplification involves retaining the core parts but in a simplified structure without changing the fundamental components.

examples.chinese: 我的脚很痛(我的腳很痛)。
他用脚踢球(他用腳踢球)。
examples.english: He kicks the ball with his foot.

examples.chinese: 在沙滩上走路时(在沙灘上走路時)，脚会陷入沙子里(腳會陷入沙子裡)。
examples.pinyin: Zài shātān shàng zǒulù shí, jiǎo huì xiàn rù shāzi lǐ.
examples.english: When walking on the beach, your feet sink into the sand.
